target_behavior: Return stable inode and accurate errno for virtual and physical paths
notes: posix_phase1_tests covers mount root, /skills, skill dir, compiled SKILL.md, passthrough file (stable inode across repeated stats), and missing-path ENOENT under root, /skills, and skill dir

target_behavior: Project real mode,uid,gid,nlink,ctime,blocks,rdev where physical; document virtual attrs
notes: Physical file attrs now use Unix MetadataExt fields; virtual attrs remain deterministic

target_behavior: Expose symlink identity without following target
notes: lookup/getattr now use symlink_metadata; FileType::Symlink projected; posix_phase1_tests covers lstat vs stat divergence and readdir DT_LNK

target_behavior: Return link target bytes for symlink in physical namespace
notes: FUSE readlink callback returns raw target bytes; posix_phase1_tests covers passthrough, missing-path ENOENT, and virtual-path EINVAL

target_behavior: Mode changes pass through for physical files and dirs; virtual paths reject accurately

target_behavior: Ownership changes pass through when permitted; EPERM preserved

target_behavior: Check R_OK/W_OK/X_OK/F_OK according to projected permissions
notes: Physical access uses source metadata and request uid/gid; virtual dirs deny W_OK; virtual skill-discover files deny W_OK/X_OK

target_behavior: Return usable filesystem capacity and limits from source filesystem

target_behavior: Open existing file read-only only if readable and not directory
notes: fd-backed open now validates common path and access-mode cases

target_behavior: Respect access mode and path type
notes: Virtual compiled SKILL.md read/write policy is explicit; skill-discover remains read-only

target_behavior: Truncate physical file and sync SKILL.md store
notes: Includes Linux-compatible O_RDONLY|O_TRUNC behavior for physical files and SKILL.md

target_behavior: Writes append regardless of supplied offset for append handle
notes: fd-backed append handles covered by POSIX open/IO tests

target_behavior: Offset reads return requested bytes or EOF without loading whole large file

target_behavior: Write reparses and updates store with directory name as key

target_behavior: Reject invalid offsets with EINVAL and avoid casts to huge u64
notes: posix_phase1_tests covers negative pread returning EINVAL, large valid read past EOF returning 0, and sparse pwrite at offset 100 preserving size and hole behavior; EFBIG is not exercised in Phase 1

target_behavior: Return pending close-time write errors where available
notes: Current boundary validates known handles and succeeds as safe no-op; pending write-error tracking remains a possible refinement

target_behavior: sync_all/sync_data on physical fd; virtual paths handled deterministically

target_behavior: Read/write via mmap behaves consistently with read/write path

target_behavior: Directory handles are tracked and released
notes: opendir snapshots entries; releasedir frees handle; fh shared counter avoids collision

target_behavior: Offsets are stable across multiple getdents calls

target_behavior: Mutation during iteration does not duplicate/skip within opened dir stream

target_behavior: Pass through user.* xattrs on ordinary passthrough leaves; deterministic EOPNOTSUPP on virtual paths and non-user.* namespaces; listxattr filtered to user.* so non-user names never leak
notes: posix_xattr_tests exercises real lgetxattr/llistxattr round trips against an xattr-capable temp root (SKILLFS_XATTR_TEST_ROOT > <workspace>/target/xattr-tests > $HOME/.cache); positive tests skip with an explicit message when no candidate substrate honors user.*

target_behavior: Set/remove user.* xattrs with flags semantics on ordinary passthrough leaves; .skill-meta and lifecycle reserved roots return EACCES via PolicyDenied; virtual paths and non-user.* namespaces return EOPNOTSUPP with Metadata Rejected audit class=virtual_xattr_path / class=unsupported_xattr_namespace
notes: Uses no-follow lsetxattr/lremovexattr; the kernel VFS may pre-empt trusted.*/security.* mutations with EPERM for non-CAP_SYS_ADMIN callers and that is accepted; audit JSONL field shape unchanged (Metadata kind with class/verb/name in existing detail string)

notes: fuser has lseek callback

notes: Useful for cp on modern Linux

notes: watcher.rs exists but is not connected

notes: Currently loaded once at mount

target_behavior: Writes preserve categorized layout and store category metadata

target_behavior: All P0 operations pass in normal and in-place modes
notes: posix_phase1_tests parity_smoke runs SKILL.md read + write/read round trip + chmod + statfs in both modes; write_guard_tests covers mkdir/rename invariants in both modes

target_behavior: SIGTERM/Ctrl-C unmount cleanly and no stale mount remains

target_behavior: Default-on image has clear fallback when /dev/fuse or fusermount3 missing

target_behavior: Keep .skill-meta/** read-visible but deny normal mutations with EACCES
notes: S1 adds lexical classifier + default SkillMetaProtectionPolicy; posix_phase1_tests cover read/stat/readdir visibility, create/open/write/truncate/unlink/rmdir/mkdir/rename/setattr/access W_OK denial, non-meta passthrough success, skill-discover bypass, and unchanged symlink EROFS

target_behavior: Best-effort JSONL audit sink with stable field names; default remains NoopEventSink; sink failures must not change syscall outcomes
notes: S2 adds security/audit.rs (AuditConfig, JsonlFileAuditSink, serialize_event_jsonl) and representative emission for open/create/write/read failure/metadata plus existing policy/readlink/symlink/link/delete/rename events. 9 unit tests pin JSONL shape, drop counter, file-write success, and unwritable-path error. 4 integration tests in audit_event_stream_tests.rs exercise a live mount with InMemoryEventSink and JsonlFileAuditSink, including S1 PolicyDenied attribution, Allowed Open/Create/Write events on passthrough, unchanged errno semantics with sink attached, and on-disk JSONL parse round-trip

target_behavior: CLI/runtime opt-in for the JSONL audit sink: --audit-log enables it, --audit-queue-capacity tunes the bounded channel (0 maps to DEFAULT_AUDIT_QUEUE_CAPACITY), default keeps NoopEventSink, and an unwritable explicit audit path aborts mount at startup instead of downgrading silently
notes: S2.1 adds AuditRuntimeConfig and build_sink() in security/audit.rs plus the blocking mount_with_security entry point. CLI mount subcommand grows --audit-log and --audit-queue-capacity flags. 6 new unit tests in security::audit::tests cover disabled→None, enabled→writes JSONL, invalid path→Err, zero/explicit capacity normalization, and is_enabled. 4 new integration tests in audit_runtime_tests.rs drive the same helper through mount_background_with_security: default config produces no log file, enabled path records a policy_denied JSONL line, invalid path returns startup error before mounting, and zero queue capacity is normalized end-to-end. audit_event_stream_tests, posix_phase1_tests, posix_open_io_tests, and write_guard_tests stay green.

target_behavior: Startup-time gate that --security-mode requires source and mountpoint to canonicalize to the same directory (in-place layout). Reject non-in-place pairs with a clear startup error before audit setup or mountpoint auto-creation. Default off; compat (non-in-place) layout still works with a strengthened warning that direct source writes bypass SkillFS policy/audit and that drift observation only runs when --audit-log is enabled.
notes: M0 adds security/mode.rs with SecurityModeConfig and SecurityModeError plus the validate(source, mountpoint) primitive. CLI mount subcommand grows --security-mode flag; cmd_mount runs validate first (validate → build_sink → mountpoint create → mount) so a rejected security mount leaves no startup side effects. 12 unit tests in security::mode::tests cover disabled default, identical-path acceptance, canonicalize-equal acceptance via . suffix, distinct-directory rejection, missing-source/missing-mountpoint canonicalize errors, error-message content, and io::Error chain preservation. 7 integration tests in security_mode_runtime_tests.rs drive the same helper through mount_background_with_security: security mode accepts in-place mount and serves passthrough I/O, rejects non-in-place pre-mount, default compat still works with the source path remaining directly writable, security mode composes with AuditRuntimeConfig::enabled producing the same policy_denied JSONL on an S1 .skill-meta denial, and an invalid audit path aborts the mount before any FUSE work in both security mode and compat mode. .skill-meta enforcement, audit JSONL shape, SKILL.md compiled read/write sync, skill-discover virtual semantics, and POSIX errno paths are unchanged.

target_behavior: Visibility-only source drift observer that converts out-of-band source-tree observations into SourceChanged audit events through the existing SkillEventSink pipeline. Default no-op; not wired into FUSE callbacks or runtime watcher yet.
notes: W0 adds security/drift.rs with DriftChangeKind, DriftScope, DriftEvent, SourceDriftObserver, and classify_drift_path plus SkillEventKind::SourceChanged mapped to JSONL kind source_changed. Existing JSONL field names are unchanged; drift-specific metadata uses detail shape change=<kind> scope=<scope> path=<observed>. The lexical classifier mirrors the core parser skill-name shape, refuses invalid top-level components, and refuses attribution for categorized manifest paths such as <source>/<category>/<skill>/SKILL.md; store-aware producers can pre-classify via DriftEvent::with_scope. 75 lib tests and 4 source_drift_observer integration tests pass; the live mount test proves observer emission does not affect passthrough reads/writes. No policy enforcement, quarantine, lifecycle behavior, store hot sync, or new POSIX surface is added.

target_behavior: Runtime watcher wiring for W0 drift observation. When --audit-log is enabled, SkillFS starts the existing source watcher before the FUSE mount and emits source_changed audit events for SKILL.md and immediate skill-dir drift. Default no-audit behavior remains no-op.
notes: W1 adds security/drift_runtime.rs, watcher readiness signaling in skillfs-core::watcher, CLI wiring gated by --audit-log, and AuditRuntimeConfig::validate_audit_path_outside_source to reject audit logs inside the source tree before sink creation. Coverage intentionally mirrors core watcher output only: <source>/<skill>/SKILL.md create/modify/delete and immediate <source>/<skill> dir create/delete; arbitrary files inside skills, .skill-meta/**, top-level files, and nested/category layouts are not observed. 90 lib tests and 9 drift_runtime integration tests pass; full skillfs-fuse is 212 green and skillfs-core is 85 passed with watcher ignored tests unchanged. No store hot sync, view reload, enforcement, quarantine, lifecycle behavior, or new POSIX surface is added. W1.1 later adds the explicit watcher shutdown handle for long-lived embedders.

target_behavior: Explicit shutdown handle for W1 source drift observation. Long-lived embedders can stop and await the underlying notify watcher and drift adapter deterministically; CLI signal and mount-exit paths use the same shutdown path. Watcher coverage and default no-audit behavior are unchanged.
notes: W1.1 adds skillfs-core::watcher::WatcherHandle and watch_source_with_handle(), preserves the original watch_source() receiver-only API, and changes security::drift_runtime::spawn_drift_watcher() to return DriftWatcherHandle. DriftWatcherHandle::shutdown() closes the core watcher first, then awaits the adapter after the event channel closes. CLI Ctrl+C, SIGTERM, and natural mount-exit branches call shutdown().await; no --audit-log still starts no watcher. 4 new core watcher unit tests and 2 new drift_runtime integration tests cover readiness, synchronous missing-source failure, explicit shutdown without filesystem events, and repeated start/stop cycles. Validation: cargo check -p skillfs -p skillfs-fuse, cargo build --workspace, cargo test -p skillfs-core (89 passed, watcher ignored unchanged), cargo test -p skillfs-fuse --test drift_runtime_tests (11 passed), and cargo test -p skillfs-fuse (215 green). No broader watcher coverage, store hot sync, view reload, enforcement, quarantine, lifecycle behavior, or new POSIX surface is added.

target_behavior: Reserve .staging, .certified, .quarantine, and .archive as hidden lifecycle namespace roots for future security workflows. Ordinary lookup/readdir hides these roots; mutations targeting them are denied before physical I/O. Neighbour and nested names remain ordinary.
notes: S3 adds security/lifecycle.rs with exact case-sensitive reserved-name helpers and registers the surface from security.rs. lib.rs hides reserved roots from lookup and both dynamic/opendir readdir snapshots, and gates mkdir, create, mutating open, write, unlink, rmdir, rename from/to, and mutating setattr before source writes. Existing PolicyDenied event shape is reused; no new JSONL fields or event kinds are added. lifecycle_namespace_tests.rs adds 20 FUSE integration tests across normal and in-place mounts, covering ENOENT visibility, EACCES/ENOENT mutation boundaries, source preservation, neighbour names such as .staging2, nested paths such as alpha/docs/.staging and alpha/.staging, ordinary skill mkdir, and skill-discover invariants. 6 lifecycle unit tests bring skillfs-fuse lib tests to 96 passed. Validation also keeps POSIX phase1/open_io, write_guard, audit, drift, security-mode, and skillfs-core green. No lifecycle transitions, management view, quarantine/scanner integration, ledger, trusted identity, capability enforcement, watcher hot sync, view reload, or new POSIX surface is added.
